mappings.rationale: Directly covers crystal structures, symmetry, diffraction methods, crystal growth, and crystallographic classification systems central to this section.
Directly covers the electronic structure of solids, band theory, superconductivity, magnetic phenomena, and semiconductor physics discussed throughout this section.
Directly covers superconductivity in metals including transition temperature, critical field, Meissner effect, and energy gaps discussed in this section.
Directly covers paramagnetism, diamagnetism, ferromagnetism, antiferromagnetism, and ferrimagnetism in solids as discussed in this section.
Covers the elastic and plastic properties of solids, defects, thermal properties, and the mechanical behaviour of crystalline and amorphous materials.
Covers the solid state of matter including crystal structure, bonding types, metals, insulators, and semiconductors discussed in this section.
Relevant to the glassy or amorphous state of matter, polymeric solids, and the transition between crystalline and non-crystalline states.
Covers semiconductor device principles, surface phenomena, and nanoscale properties of solids relevant to this section.
Provides coverage of solid-state physics including crystal structure, band theory, and semiconductor physics discussed in this section.
Covers the quantum mechanical foundations underlying energy-band theory, electron behaviour in solids, and semiconductor physics.
Covers ionic crystals, crystal structures, bonding in crystalline minerals, and defects in crystal structures discussed in this section.
Relevant to the interaction of light with solids, the photoelectric effect, photoemission, and optical properties of crystals discussed in this section.
Covers the historical development of solid-state physics including crystallography, band theory, and superconductivity.
Relevant to the properties of metals, semiconductors, and insulators as they relate to electron configuration and periodic trends.
Directly relevant to symmetry considerations in crystal classification, the 14 Bravais lattices, and point group symmetries in this section.
Relevant to electron diffraction and imaging techniques used to study crystal structures and defects in solids.
Relevant to crystalline and amorphous minerals, the structure of geological solids, and crystal growth processes.
Covers bonding theory in ionic and covalent solids, crystal energetics, and the thermodynamic properties of solid systems.
Covers the classification of solids by bonding type and the chemical properties of ionic, metallic, and covalent solids.
Relevant to the crystal structure of geological materials and the formation of mineral crystals discussed in this section.
